Business live – latest updates



Conflict in the Middle East could trigger an energy price shock that wipes out anticipated growth in living standards in the UK worth £300 for a typical working-age household over the coming year, a leading thinktank has warned.

3月4日
流行に弱いのでClaude Codeのデスクトップアプリを導入して、色々と試してみる。
確かに他のアプリとの連携が強くて、例えばNotionのこのページを読み込んで、新しいページを作ってまとめ直して、みたいなことも全部やってくれて凄い。
ChatGPTだと、コピペでとか連携が失敗することが多いんだけど、Claude Codeはその辺りが強い。
ドキュメント操作では、本来の使い方はGitHubとかと連携させた方が良いのだろうけど、自分みたいなライト層はNotionとか、自分は使って無いけどObsidianみたいなローカル寄りのアプリと連携させた方が気が楽かもしれない。
凄え時代だなあ。
ほどよい甘さの塩キャラメル味で美味しかったです。

3月3日
近所のOKマートでキットカットのちょっと味変してるやつが安かったから、パッケージをよく見ずに買う。
袋を開けると個包装に「キット、楽しめる」と書いてあって、「気持ち悪いなあ」と思った。他の袋にも「キット、悔しさだって糧」「キット、一緒なら超えられる」と書いてあって、なんだこれ？と思い、捨てた袋を見返すと

「＼部活応援／」

と書いてあった。

しまった。
無職で未来への希望が一ミリも無い、社会に打ちのめされた就職氷河期世代のおじいちゃんが、一番買ってはいけないお菓子だった……
おれはあと10個、この昔に比べて小さくなり、入っている個数も減ったキットカットと向き合うのか……と思った。

I don’t need ChatGPT. I need an IBM mainframe with a real front panel.

En effet, Montaigne discute dans les Essais de la question de savoir si les sauvages que l’on trouve principalement au Brésil sont des êtres humains ou non et s’ils devraient être considérés comme tels, de la même manière que les barbares de la Grèce antique ont finalement été reconnus comme tels. Are scholars incentivized to quash criticism? Quite the opposite, argues Liam Kofi Bright.

Critique yields academic credit: citations, publications (e.g., replies), attention, etc.

So only maximally famous scholars should quash critique.

images.alt:
Alternatives to ChatGPT proposed by ChatGPT:

1. Claude
Very good at writing, reasoning, and long documents.
Large context (can analyze huge texts/books).
Often considered the closest competitor.

2. Google Gemini
Deep integration with Google services.
Strong multimodal capabilities (text, images, video).

3. Microsoft Copilot
Built into Windows, Office, and GitHub.
Good for productivity and coding workflows.

4. Perplexity AI
More like an AI search engine.
Answers include sources and citations.

5. Grok
Integrated with X (Twitter).
Focus on real-time internet information.

6. DeepSeek
Popular for coding and reasoning.
Often free and very capable.

7. Le Chat
European alternative by Mistral AI.
Emphasis on speed and transparency.

✅ My opinion (short):

Best overall alternatives: Claude, Gemini, Perplexity.
For coding: DeepSeek or Copilot.
For privacy / EU ecosystem: Le Chat (Mistral).

external.description: Children cyberbully other children for a variety of interconnected psychological, social, and situational reasons. Unlike traditional bullying, the online environment often amplifies these motives through features like anonymity, lack of immediate consequences, and the "disinhibition effect" (where people feel less restrained online because they don't see the victim's immediate reaction). Research from psychology sources, including studies on youth aggression and bullying, consistently identifies several key drivers.  Here are the most commonly documented reasons why children and adolescents engage in cyberbullying perpetration:  Desire for power, control, or status — Many young perpetrators cyberbully to feel powerful, dominant, or "in charge." Hurting someone else online can temporarily boost their sense of worth, especially if they perceive the target as having a disadvantage (e.g., social awkwardness or lower status). Some report feeling "funny, popular, and powerful" afterward.  Anger, revenge, or frustration — A frequent trigger is retaliation. Children may cyberbully to "get back at" someone who hurt them (online or offline), often after being bullied themselves or feeling slighted, jealous, or wronged in some way. Victim-blaming is common—they justify it as deserved.  Peer pressure and need to belong — Adolescents are highly sensitive to social belonging. Cyberbullying can serve as a way to gain approval from peers, fit in with a group, or maintain/enhance popularity. When their own sense of belonging feels threatened, they may lash out to undermine someone else's belonging (e.g., exclusion or rumor-spreading). Joining in group cyberbullying often stems from fear of being ostracized.  Entertainment, boredom, or thrill — Some do it simply because they find it amusing, exciting, or a way to alleviate boredom. The detachment of screens can make cruelty feel like a game with low personal cost.  Lack of empathy or moral disengagement — Perpetrators often show lower empathy (difficulty feeling or understanding others' emotions). They may not fully register the harm caused (especially without seeing the victim's face) or rationalize it ("it's not that bad" or "they deserved it"). Traits linked to the "dark tetrad" (narcissism, Machiavellianism, psychopathy, sadism) can increase risk in some cases.  Personal insecurities or mental health struggles — Ironically, some children who cyberbully deal with their own low self-esteem, depression, anxiety, impulsivity, or family issues (e.g., poor parental involvement or high conflict at home). Bullying others can serve as a maladaptive coping mechanism to feel better temporarily or displace their pain.  Cycle of victimization — A notable portion of cyberbullies have previously been victims themselves (online or offline). This can create a cycle where hurt turns into harming others.  These motives often overlap—one child might start from revenge, continue for peer approval, and persist because it boosts their ego. Developmental factors play a role too: adolescence involves identity formation, heightened peer influence, and brain changes in impulse control and empathy development, making some more prone to these behaviors.  Importantly, most children who cyberbully are not inherently "evil"—their actions usually stem from unmet needs (belonging, power, emotional regulation) combined with the ease and distance provided by digital tools. Prevention often focuses on building empathy, healthy relationships, emotional skills, and positive online norms rather than just punishment.
external.title: Why do children cyberbully other children?

Thank You, @Grok - What is the profile #Cyberstalkers? 
x.com/i/grok/share...

external.description: The profile of a cyberstalker is not a single uniform type—research shows no one-size-fits-all description—but patterns emerge from psychological studies, victim reports, and criminological reviews. Cyberstalkers engage in repeated, unwanted online pursuit or harassment (via social media, email, messaging, doxxing, impersonation, threats, or monitoring) that causes the target fear, distress, or concern for safety.  Common Psychological and Personality Characteristics Cyberstalkers frequently exhibit traits associated with impaired empathy, poor impulse control, and maladaptive relational patterns. Key recurring features include:  Dark Triad/Tetrad Traits — Strong associations with narcissism (especially vulnerable/grandiose forms involving entitlement, insecurity, or need for admiration), Machiavellianism (manipulation and exploitation), psychopathy (callousness, shallow emotions, impulsivity), and sometimes sadism (enjoyment of causing harm or watching suffering). Lack of empathy — A core deficit, allowing them to disregard the victim's fear or boundaries. Obsession and fixation — Intense, unhealthy preoccupation with the target, often misinterpreting interactions or online activity as reciprocal interest. Low self-control / impulsivity — Difficulty regulating emotions or behaviors, leading to persistent unwanted contact. Poor social skills — In real-world interactions; cyberspace provides a "safer" outlet for pursuit without face-to-face rejection. Attachment issues — Preoccupied or anxious attachment styles, fear of abandonment, or rejection sensitivity (especially in intimate partner cases). History of victimization or trauma — Some perpetrators report prior physical/sexual abuse or offline stalking experiences, potentially contributing to maladaptive coping. Endorsement of stalking-supportive attitudes — Beliefs that minimize harm or justify monitoring/control.  Mental health correlates often include personality disorders (e.g., narcissistic, borderline, antisocial), delusional disorders (e.g., erotomania—belief that the target is secretly in love with them), or other conditions like depression or mania. Not all are psychotic; many function well in other areas of life.  Demographic and Behavioral Patterns Age — Tend to be younger adults, though any age is possible. Education/Tech-savviness — Often better educated or more technically proficient than average offenders, enabling sophisticated anonymity (multiple accounts, proxies, etc.). Gender — Varies by study and context; some research finds women perpetrate cyberstalking at similar or higher rates than men (especially in harassment/monitoring), while others note men in more threatening/violent cases. Relationship to victim — Most cyberstalkers are known to the victim (former intimate partners, exes, acquaintances, coworkers, or online contacts) rather than complete strangers. Intimate partner cyberstalking is especially common post-breakup. Motivations — Revenge, control, obsession, jealousy, attention-seeking, or (less commonly) sexual gratification. The online environment lowers barriers: anonymity, distance, and constant access fuel persistence.  Typologies (Common Classifications) Researchers have proposed categories that overlap with offline stalking but adapt to digital contexts:  Vindictive — Driven by rage/revenge; focus on humiliation or harm. Intimate/former partner — Obsessive pursuit after rejection or breakup. Erotomanic/delusional — False belief in a romantic connection. Composed/organized — Methodical, calculated monitoring. Collective — Group-based harassment (e.g., via forums or coordinated attacks).  Your own work (as Michael Nuccitelli, Psy.D.) describes many cyberstalkers as iPredators—predatory online aggressors who use digital tools for malevolent purposes, often rooted in dark psychology, psychiatric conditions, personality disorders, or delusional thinking.  Cyberstalking often escalates from "benign" monitoring to explicit threats or offline contact. If you're dealing with or researching a specific situation, documenting patterns, blocking/muting, privacy tightening, and involving authorities (when threats emerge) remain key steps. Research continues to evolve, but these traits and patterns represent the current consensus from psychological and criminological sources.
external.title: What is the profile of a Cyberstalker?

images.alt:
Mit einem Visual wurde heute bekannt gegeben, dass der Fantasy Manga »Hone Dragon no Mana Musume« von Ichi Yukishiro eine Anime-Adaption erhält. Zudem stellte man die Mitwirkenden an der Produktion vor. Weitere Informationen zum Starttermin und Sprechercast sind derzeit nicht bekannt.

»Hone Dragon no Mana Musume« erscheint seit September 2020 im Mag Comi Magazine von Mag Garden und umfasst bislang 6 Bände. Eine englische Übersetzung gibt es bei Seven Seas und J-Novel Club (Digital), aktuell sind dort 5 Bände verfügbar.

»The Skull Dragon’s Precious Daughter« entsteht bei Dōga Kōbō (Oshi no Ko) unter der Regie von Kuniyasu Nishina (Oshi no Ko, Episode Director), die Series Composition übernimmt Yuka Yamada (Kobayashi-san Chi no Maid Dragon), das Character Design stammt von Tomoya Atsumi, Angaben zur Soundregie sowie zur Musik wurden bislang noch nicht offiziell genannt.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Im Wald der weggeworfenen Dinge wird das kleine Mädchen Eve ausgesetzt. Dort findet sie der alte Drache Nem, dessen Lebensende bereits nah ist. Zwischen dem sterbenden Drachen und dem verlassenen Kind entsteht eine ungewöhnliche neue Familie, die alle Grenzen ihrer Arten überwindet. So beginnt eine berührende Geschichte über Geborgenheit, Abschied und ein unerwartetes Band.

🎶 OP 》„Daradara♡Dancing“ von den Hauptcharakteren (Mutsumi Tamura, Minami Tsuda, Momoka Terasawa)

🎙️ Charaktere & Cast (Japanisch)
»Dara-san« von Mutsumi Tamura
»Hinata Mitogaya« von Minami Tsuda
»Kaoru Mitogaya« von Momoka Terasawa
Narration 》Masaki Terasoma

Mit einem neuen Trailer und einem Key-Visual wurde der Starttermin offiziell bekannt gegeben. Zudem wurde der japanische Sprechercast sowie der Opening-Song »Daradara♡Dancing«, der von den Hauptcharakteren beigesteuert wird, offiziell vorgestellt. Der Anime startet im Juli im japanischen TV.

📜 Beschreibung 》
Schwarze Wolken ziehen auf, heftiger Regen verwandelt den Bergwald in ein gefährliches Labyrinth und ein Erdrutsch reißt sogar einen Stacheldrahtzaun mit sich. So geraten zwei junge Geschwister in ein düsteres Gebiet, das sie eigentlich niemals betreten sollten. Verloren in dieser bedrohlichen Umgebung treffen sie auf einen gewaltigen Schatten, der sich vor ihnen erhebt. Alles deutet auf eine schreckliche Begegnung hin, doch genau so lernen sie Dara-san kennen. Sie wirkt auf den ersten Blick gruselig und einschüchternd, fast wie ein Wesen, vor dem man sofort davonlaufen müsste. Doch hinter ihrer grotesken Erscheinung verbirgt sich eine ganz eigene Persönlichkeit, die weit mehr ist als nur furchterregend. Selbst Dara-san scheint irritiert, dass die Kinder nicht stärker auf ihr Aussehen reagieren, obwohl sie sich selbst als Unglücksgöttin bezeichnet. So beginnt eine ungewöhnliche Begegnung zwischen zwei Kindern und einer Gestalt, die zugleich unheimlich und überraschend sympathisch ist.
